name: Riboflavin (B2) sensor (FMN riboswitch + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects riboflavin / FMN (vitamin B2) in food and the gut. The natural FMN riboswitch gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the gut-persistent probiotic Lactiplantibacillus plantarum.

input.detects: Riboflavin/FMN in food, supplements, and the gut

sensing.sensorMechanism: The natural FMN riboswitch binds flavin mononucleotide and modulates downstream expression.
sensing.signalFlow: FMN/B2 -> FMN riboswitch gates sgRNA expression -> dCas9-activator amplifies the reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence reports B2 level.

parts.name: sgRNA (riboswitch-controlled)
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak promoter

parts.name: dCas9-ω activator
parts.source: CRISPRa (Bikard et al. 2013); CRISPRi in lactobacilli

performance.notes: The FMN riboswitch is a well-characterised natural B2 sensor; integrated CRISPRa device is a design template. L. plantarum itself produces riboflavin (food-fortification relevance).

safety.biocontainment: Auxotrophy (alr/dapA) and food-grade containment; GRAS probiotic host.

buildSteps.title: Design sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target/position the sgRNA; check L. plantarum WCFS1 off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: Sensor -> sgRNA; dCas9; reporter. Use the host's standard toolkit.

buildSteps.title: Transform L. plantarum WCFS1
buildSteps.description: Transform, select, and confirm low background.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the sample plus a riboflavin standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the natural FMN riboswitch with L. plantarum CRISPRa
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
